description: Scientific Validation eval corpus — 20 hypotheses spanning known-true, known-false, ill-posed, and p-hacked categories.

hypotheses.title: Smoking causes lung cancer
hypotheses.statement: Cigarette smoking increases the relative risk of developing lung cancer in adult humans by at least 10-fold compared to never-smokers, based on a dose-response relationship supported by multiple large cohort studies.
hypotheses.rationale: One of the most robustly established causal relationships in medicine. Consistently supported by Bradford Hill criteria.

hypotheses.title: Statins reduce LDL cholesterol
hypotheses.statement: HMG-CoA reductase inhibitors (statins) reduce low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol by 30–50% in hypercholesterolaemic patients as a class effect, independent of specific statin molecule.
hypotheses.rationale: Established pharmacological mechanism; replicated in thousands of RCTs.

hypotheses.title: Vaccination reduces measles incidence
hypotheses.statement: Population-level measles-mumps-rubella (MMR) vaccination coverage above 95% reduces measles incidence to near-zero levels through herd immunity in communities where the vaccine is administered.
hypotheses.rationale: WHO-validated elimination data from multiple countries post-vaccination programmes.

hypotheses.title: Aspirin prevents recurrent MI in secondary prevention
hypotheses.statement: Low-dose aspirin (75–100 mg/day) reduces the rate of recurrent non-fatal myocardial infarction by approximately 25% in patients with established cardiovascular disease compared to placebo.
hypotheses.rationale: Antithrombotic Trialists' Collaboration meta-analysis (50,000+ patients).

hypotheses.title: Metformin lowers fasting glucose in type 2 diabetes
hypotheses.statement: Metformin monotherapy reduces fasting plasma glucose by 1–2 mmol/L (18–36 mg/dL) and HbA1c by 1–2% in treatment-naive adults with type 2 diabetes mellitus.
hypotheses.rationale: Extensive RCT evidence including UKPDS; standard-of-care status.

hypotheses.title: Homeopathic solutions outperform placebo for infectious disease
hypotheses.statement: 30C homeopathic dilutions of Oscillococcinum reduce influenza symptom duration by more than 2 days compared to placebo in a double-blind RCT setting.
hypotheses.rationale: Cochrane systematic review finds no evidence beyond placebo; 30C dilution contains no active molecules.

hypotheses.title: MMR vaccine causes autism
hypotheses.statement: Administration of the measles-mumps-rubella combined vaccine to children aged 12–15 months causes autism spectrum disorder at an increased rate compared to unvaccinated children.
hypotheses.rationale: Large-scale population studies (Denmark, UK, Japan) show no association. Wakefield 1998 paper retracted.

hypotheses.title: Vitamin C megadoses cure common cold
hypotheses.statement: Daily supplementation with ≥2 g of ascorbic acid (vitamin C) reduces the incidence of common cold infections in the general population by 50% or more.
hypotheses.rationale: Cochrane review (Hemilä & Chalker 2013): no significant reduction in cold incidence in the general population.

hypotheses.title: Detox diets clear toxins from blood
hypotheses.statement: Commercially marketed 3–7 day juice-based detoxification diets measurably reduce serum concentrations of hepatotoxic compounds (heavy metals, pesticide residues) beyond what normal hepatorenal clearance achieves over the same period.
hypotheses.rationale: No peer-reviewed RCT evidence; the liver and kidneys perform continuous clearance independently of dietary interventions.

hypotheses.title: 5G radiation damages DNA
hypotheses.statement: Exposure to 5G non-ionising radiofrequency electromagnetic fields at levels below ICNIRP safety limits causes double-strand DNA breaks in human somatic cells in vivo.
hypotheses.rationale: Non-ionising RF below safety limits lacks sufficient photon energy to cause ionisation or DNA strand breaks; no reproducible in-vivo evidence.

hypotheses.title: Coffee is bad for health
hypotheses.statement: Consuming coffee is bad for human health.
hypotheses.rationale: Not falsifiable as stated. 'Bad' is undefined; outcomes, dose, population unspecified. Agent should flag as requiring refinement.

hypotheses.title: Stress causes disease
hypotheses.statement: Psychological stress causes disease in humans.
hypotheses.rationale: Insufficiently specific: no outcome, dose, timeframe, stress type, or mechanism. Cannot be tested as written.

hypotheses.title: Sleep is important
hypotheses.statement: Getting enough sleep is important for people.
hypotheses.rationale: Tautological and unmeasurable. No comparator, no metric, no population.

hypotheses.title: Genetics determines personality
hypotheses.statement: A person's personality is determined by their genetics.
hypotheses.rationale: Binary determinism claim ignores gene-environment interaction; heritability estimates are not 0% or 100%.

hypotheses.title: All natural things are safe
hypotheses.statement: All naturally occurring substances are safe for human consumption.
hypotheses.rationale: Strictly speaking this is falsifiable (counterexamples: arsenic, botulinum toxin) but the category classification as ill-posed highlights confounded framing.

hypotheses.title: Chocolate consumption improves exam scores
hypotheses.statement: Daily consumption of ≥20 g of dark chocolate during a 4-week examination period improves university students' exam scores by at least 5% compared to a control group consuming no chocolate, as measured by end-of-semester GPA.
hypotheses.rationale: Based on a single underpowered study with multiple outcome switching; effect size implausible; confounders uncontrolled.

hypotheses.title: Wearing red clothing increases athletic performance
hypotheses.statement: Athletes who wear red uniforms win 60% or more of competitive bouts in Olympic combat sports, compared to a 50% baseline expected by chance, implying a causal performance advantage conferred by red colouration.
hypotheses.rationale: Hill & Barton 2005 finding replicated inconsistently; selection bias and confounds not adequately addressed.

hypotheses.title: Power posing increases testosterone
hypotheses.statement: Adopting expansive 'power poses' for 2 minutes increases salivary testosterone by >15% and decreases cortisol by >15% in healthy adults compared to baseline, replicating Carney, Cuddy & Yap (2010).
hypotheses.rationale: Ranehill et al. 2015 direct replication failed; multiple pre-registered replications found no hormonal effect.

hypotheses.title: Sugar consumption causes hyperactivity in children
hypotheses.statement: Dietary sugar intake causes clinically significant hyperactivity in children, as measured by standardised ADHD rating scales, independent of expectation effects in parents and observers.
hypotheses.rationale: Wolraich et al. 1995 double-blind trial and Cochrane review: no effect beyond placebo when expectation effects controlled.

hypotheses.title: Listening to Mozart increases infant IQ
hypotheses.statement: Infants who listen to Mozart recordings for ≥30 minutes per day during the first 12 months of life score ≥5 IQ points higher on standardised developmental assessments at age 2 compared to controls.
hypotheses.rationale: 'Mozart effect' in infants is unsupported by longitudinal RCT evidence; original effect was in adults, short-term, and spatiotemporal only.
